description: Fix card content scrolling so that when content exceeds MAX_CARD_HEIGHT, the card displays a visible scrollbar and users can scroll through all content directly within the card. The current implementation has conflicting overflow properties that prevent reliable scrolling.
steps: In src/App.jsx at line ~4765, change the outer card container's overflow from 'hidden' to 'visible' or remove it entirely. The current code `...(nodeDims.height >= MAX_CARD_HEIGHT ? { maxHeight: nodeDims.height, overflow: 'hidden' } : {})` clips the scrollbar of child elements. Change it to just set maxHeight without overflow:hidden, or change overflow to 'auto' on the outer container and remove the inner scroll container approach.

In src/App.jsx at line ~4866, change the content wrapper div from `className="mt-2 flex-1 overflow-hidden"` to `className="mt-2 flex-1 min-h-0"` (or `overflow-y-auto`). The `overflow-hidden` class on this wrapper prevents scrolling from reaching the inner content div. Using `min-h-0` allows flex children to shrink below their content size, enabling overflow-y-auto on the inner div to work.

In src/App.jsx at line ~4880-4881, ensure the scroll container div reliably becomes scrollable: (1) Keep the `overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar` classes when nodeDims.height >= MAX_CARD_HEIGHT. (2) Change the maxHeight calculation from `nodeDims.height - 80` to a value that accounts for the title and padding properly (e.g., `nodeDims.height - 60` or use `calc` based on available space). The key is that the container must have both overflow-y:auto AND a constrained maxHeight to trigger scrolling.

In src/index.css, make the custom scrollbar more visible by increasing the width from 4px to 6px and using a slightly more opaque color for the thumb to clearly communicate to users that the content is scrollable. Consider adding a subtle fade/gradient indicator at the bottom of the scrollable area.

Ensure the outer card container uses `overflow: visible` or `overflow: hidden` only on the x-axis (overflow-x: hidden) so the scrollbar on the y-axis content area is not clipped. The card should use `display: flex; flex-direction: column` which it already does via the `flex flex-col` classes, so the content area can grow to fill available space and then scroll.
acceptance_criteria: When a card's content exceeds MAX_CARD_HEIGHT (800px), a visible scrollbar appears on the content area
Users can scroll through all card content using the scrollbar or scroll gesture
The scrollbar is clearly visible (not hidden or clipped by parent overflow:hidden)
Markdown-rendered content (nested elements like lists, tables, code blocks) scrolls correctly within the container
Plain text content also scrolls correctly when exceeding the max height
The card title remains fixed/visible at the top while content scrolls beneath it
The application builds successfully with `npx vite build`
verification: Run `npx vite build` in /projects/sandbox/mind-map and confirm it succeeds without errors

Inspect the changes to confirm: (1) outer card no longer has overflow:hidden that would clip scrollbar, (2) content wrapper allows overflow to pass through, (3) inner content div has overflow-y:auto and a constrained maxHeight when content exceeds MAX_CARD_HEIGHT, (4) scrollbar CSS is more visible
findings: All changes applied cleanly. Build passes. Three key changes: (1) outer card container uses overflowX:hidden + overflowY:visible instead of overflow:hidden to avoid clipping the scrollbar, (2) content wrapper uses min-h-0 instead of overflow-hidden to allow flex children to shrink and enable inner scrolling, (3) scrollbar made more visible with 6px width and darker thumb color.
